notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 3, Book 16 source file.
Advanced card: longest texts and/or more complex sentence structure.

book.title: The Bundle of Sticks
book.description: The Bundle of Sticks includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: longest texts and/or more complex sentence structure

weeks.title: The Bundle of Sticks Book Card
vocabulary: 1. accumulated - gathered or collected over time
2. divisive - causing disagreement and conflict between people
2. exploitation - taking unfair advantage of someone
1. conventional - traditional or usual way of doing something
1. integrity - the state of being whole and undamaged
2. resilient - able to withstand difficult conditions
1. succumbed - gave way to pressure; was defeated
1. penetrating - getting through to; having a deep effect
2. unprecedented - never done or known before; extraordinary
journal: Write about how working together makes a group stronger.
Explain the father’s lesson using details from the bundle of sticks.
writing: Write about how working together makes a group stronger.
Explain the father’s lesson using details from the bundle of sticks.
questions: 1. What had Thomas built through decades of hard work?
2. What troubling behavior did Thomas notice in his sons?
1. What did each brother believe about himself?
2. How did outside merchants take advantage of the brothers?
1. Why did Thomas decide he needed to take dramatic action?
2. How did Thomas plan to communicate his message?
1. What did Thomas ask William to do with the bundle of sticks?
2. What happened when each son tried to break the bundle?
1. What happened when Thomas untied the bundle of sticks?
2. How easily did the individual sticks break?
1. How did the sons respond to their father's demonstration?
2. What happened to the family business after the brothers learned to work together?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
